examples: Thousands of Aztec artifacts came to light during the digging of the Mexico City subway in the 1970s.
It would be unrealistic to suggest that illegal digging would stop if artifacts were sold on the open market.
Armed with crowbars and the occasional hand winch, the group rescued about 1,500 artifacts from around the city.
The ice has preserved many similar items, but the unfrozen life of these artifacts is short.
More recently, federal agents broke up a ring that was illegally importing questionable pre-Columbian artifacts.
